title: Draft manifest schema for Parquet artifact consumption
summary: Define the manifest fields that let consumers discover tables, schema versions, HF revision identity, source coverage, policy versions, recipe versions, row counts, compatibility notices, and enrichment status.
deliverables: Manifest JSON schema or typed model
acceptance_criteria: Manifest records Hugging Face repo ID and full commit SHA fields
Manifest records table paths, row counts, schema versions, policy versions, and recipe versions
Validation fails when required version or artifact path fields are missing

title: Define shared core Parquet table contracts
summary: Create the first stable core table contracts for entities, titles, external IDs, relationships, relationship evidence, facets, judgments, provenance references, and manifest linkage.
acceptance_criteria: Core tables are backward-compatible by default within a major version
Core tables expose lightweight evidence/provenance/confidence references without inline field-level provenance
Tests cover required IDs and join fields

title: Define domain profile table contracts
summary: Define independently versioned profile contracts for anime, TV, and movies while keeping cross-domain concepts in core facets where possible.
acceptance_criteria: Profiles are independently versioned
Anime remains first class without making core anime-shaped
TV and movie profiles can support meaningful v1 source paths

title: Define identity-change surface
summary: Add a table contract for merges, splits, redirects, deprecations, withdrawals, and supersessions so entity IDs remain durable within a dataset line.
acceptance_criteria: Identity change rows include old ID, new ID or IDs, change type, reason/evidence, effective snapshot, and provenance reference
Entity IDs are never reused in fixtures
Consumers can resolve deprecated IDs forward

title: Model source policy and source field policy
summary: Implemented reviewed source policy and field-level publishability policy records so source roles are enforceable at artifact write time.
deliverables: Policy fixtures for current reviewed sources
acceptance_criteria: Policy records include source role, terms/license links, review date, policy version, allowed uses, and retention rules
Tests reject fields without publishability policy when used for public artifacts
Credentials are not treated as publishability evidence

title: Add artifact publishability validation gate
summary: Artifact writers now validate source, field, and artifact policy, reject restricted public inputs, and record publishability policy versions in manifests.
acceptance_criteria: Public artifact writing fails on restricted inputs
Retrieval text and embedding inputs receive explicit publishability checks
Manifest records policy versions used for validation

title: Lock v1 source path plan for anime, TV, and movies
summary: V1 source paths are locked as manami for anime, TVmaze for TV, and Wikidata movie graph for movies; anime TV/movies do not satisfy TV/movie requirements.
acceptance_criteria: At least one meaningful source path is named for anime, TV, and movies
Anime TV series and anime movies are not counted as TV/movie source paths
Each selected source has a role and publishable-field path or a blocking review task

title: Normalize source snapshot metadata
summary: V1 now emits first-class source_snapshots, provider_runs, and source_records tables. Anime, TVmaze, and Wikidata movie build milestones write secret-free source metadata sidecars, and release readiness validates their schemas, row counts, snapshot IDs, and joins from provenance/source_records.
acceptance_criteria: Every ingested row can point to source snapshot or provider run metadata
Snapshot metadata supports scheduled refresh and partial enrichment
Provider run records do not expose secrets
Release readiness validates source_snapshots/provider_runs manifest entries and joinability

title: Implement first TV source path
summary: Completed as an executable TVmaze milestone plus local v1 artifact path through shared entities, titles, external IDs, provenance/source_records, and tv_profile.
deliverables: TV ingest adapter or normalization path
acceptance_criteria: TV path publishes titles, external IDs, provenance/evidence references, and profile fields allowed by policy
TV path is not an empty table or identifier-only stub
Tests exercise shared core consumption for TV records

title: Implement first movie source path
summary: Completed as an executable Wikidata movie milestone plus local v1 artifact path through shared entities, titles, external IDs, relationships/evidence, provenance/source_records, and movie_profile.
deliverables: Movie ingest adapter or normalization path
acceptance_criteria: Movie path publishes titles, external IDs, provenance/evidence references, and profile fields allowed by policy
Movie path is not an empty table or identifier-only stub
Tests exercise shared core consumption for movie records

title: Implement relationship type and family contract
summary: Implemented precise relationship contracts with grouping families, direction, inverse mapping behavior, evidence references, confidence dimensions, and quality flags.
acceptance_criteria: Precise relationship types are not collapsed into broad families
Directional relationships preserve direction where evidence supports it
Relationship rows include evidence and confidence references

title: Add deterministic relationship extraction recipes
summary: Implemented deterministic relationship extraction recipes for source-backed edges, with ambiguous cases left for judgment candidates rather than guessed canonical output.
acceptance_criteria: Source-backed relationships are extracted without LLMs when deterministic evidence is enough
Recipe versions are recorded in artifacts
Ambiguous cases are queued as judgment candidates rather than silently guessed

title: Define LLM judgment and judgment candidate schemas
summary: Defined structured judgment candidate and LLM judgment contracts for ambiguous identity, relationship classification, tag/facet normalization, inferred facets, conflict flags, and QA.
acceptance_criteria: Judgments record provider, model, prompt, parameters, input refs, output schema version, confidence profile, and quality flags
Judgment outputs do not directly write canonical core/profile/facet/relationship values
Invalid outputs remain non-materialized

title: Define materialization recipe contract
summary: Defined the versioned materialization gate that writes approved judgment outputs into derived sidecars with judgment and recipe references instead of mutating canonical rows.
acceptance_criteria: Materialization validates publishable inputs, structured output, evidence refs, confidence, quality flags, and target eligibility
Low-confidence or unsupported judgments do not materialize
Materialized rows retain judgment and recipe references

title: Expand local LLM prompt fixture corpus
summary: Grow the judgment fixture corpus beyond the initial benchmark case so prompt tuning and provider comparisons are meaningful for relationships and inferred facets.
acceptance_criteria: Fixture corpus covers identity, relationship, adaptation, and inferred facet cases
Local measuring-stick runs can compare prompt variants reproducibly
Provider/model benchmark results remain separate from source metadata

title: Capture Hugging Face commit SHA after publish
summary: Update publication tooling so every successful HF publish returns the full dataset repo commit SHA and supports tag-based release snapshots without self-referential manifest stamping.
acceptance_criteria: Publish results include the final full HF commit SHA and bundle upload commit SHA
Consumers can pin a full commit SHA or supported tag
Tests fail if publication tooling cannot derive HF revision identity from upload results

title: Define snapshot compatibility validation
summary: Add validation that compares snapshot manifests and schema versions to detect breaking core changes, profile version bumps, derived recipe changes, and experimental surfaces.
acceptance_criteria: Core breaking changes require major version bump
Profile and derived breaking changes are reported through manifest notices
Experimental surfaces are clearly identified

title: Harden scheduled refresh checkpoint contract
summary: Ensure scheduled refresh jobs can resume from persisted HF state and publish partial progress without treating runner cache as continuity.
acceptance_criteria: Refresh state lives with the HF dataset repo
Partial checkpoint upload and resume are tested
State records source snapshot and stable batch offsets

title: Create schema documentation from current decisions
summary: Turn the architecture decisions into contributor-facing schema docs with examples for core tables, profiles, provenance/evidence, confidence, publishability, and LLM materialization. Done means the docs-decisions documentation deliverable is complete; executable schemas, manifest validation, and source/publish contracts remain owned by their implementation lanes.
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain which tables are core/profile/derived/experimental
Docs include example joins for common consumer paths
Docs distinguish confidence from provenance

title: Draft Hugging Face dataset card plan
summary: Prepare the dataset card structure for HF publication, including scope, source policy, license posture, artifact layout, snapshot pinning, limitations, and consumer examples. Done means the dataset-card plan is drafted; publish tooling, manifest emission, source policy enforcement, and final card publication remain owned by implementation/publication lanes.
acceptance_criteria: Card states the project is a dataset, not an API/application
Card explains Parquet plus manifest consumption
Card explains source/provider policy and known limitations

title: Add consumer examples for John and Alex journeys
summary: Create small examples that show how a downstream developer would load Parquet plus manifest and use core/profile/facet/relationship surfaces for The Expanse-like and Golden Time-like use cases. Done means consumer documentation examples are present; executable query fixtures, finalized schemas, ranking recipes, and product behavior remain outside this docs lane.
deliverables: Explanation of what remains out of scope
acceptance_criteria: Examples consume files, not an API
Examples show graph/facet/retrieval surfaces without implying this repo ranks recommendations
Examples include confidence and quality signals where relevant

title: Design post-v1 similarity candidate recipe contract
summary: Prepare the post-v1 derived surface contract for similarity candidates without blocking v1 or requiring source re-ingest.
acceptance_criteria: Similarity candidates are clearly post-v1
Recipe uses existing versioned surfaces
Changes require derived table recomputation but not source re-ingest

title: Validate similarity candidates against implemented base contracts
summary: Validated the post-v1 similarity candidate contract against implemented core, relationship, materialization, provenance, and manifest surfaces without making similarity a v1 blocker.
deliverables: Schema fixture or follow-up backlog updates
acceptance_criteria: Similarity candidate fields align with implemented core, relationship, and materialization contracts
Recipe inputs remain existing versioned surfaces
Validation does not require source re-ingest or make similarity candidates a v1 blocker

title: Expose adapter candidate rejection accounting
summary: Manami/anime builds now report selected candidates, normalized records, skipped candidates, and rejection reason counts. The build writes a secret-free adapter rejection summary under source metadata and mirrors aggregate counts in provider-run notes so RCs and scheduled runs can audit source rows dropped before artifact compilation.
acceptance_criteria: Build command output distinguishes selected candidates from normalized records
Skipped candidate reasons are persisted in a local run artifact or provider-run note without exposing restricted data
Tests cover missing required fields and unsupported source URL skips

title: Add provider response cache with accurate cache-hit accounting
summary: Provider HTTP should gain a persistent response cache only when build metadata can accurately distinguish remote request count from cache-hit count in provider_runs and local audit artifacts.
deliverables: Cache hit/miss stats surfaced to build results
acceptance_criteria: Cache hits skip network calls without consuming daily request budget
ProviderRun request_count and cache_hit_count reflect runtime behavior
Tests cover cache hit, cache miss, body hash validation, and provider-specific cache scope
